diary: Woke up to perfect Sunday weather and took my time with coffee, just letting the morning be quiet. Scrolled through vintage shops online while sitting in my kitchen, that familiar itch to find something beautiful secondhand. Drove over to Red Light in Capitol Hill around ten—ended up spending way longer than I planned, running my fingers over old leather jackets and mid-century side tables. There's something about thrifting that just settles me. Grabbed a late lunch at Molly Moon's, treated myself to a scoop of their lavender honey. By early afternoon I was at Gas Works watching the lake come alive with boats and paddleboards. The sun felt incredible on my skin. I just sat there on the grass for a couple hours, no phone, no rush. Felt like I remembered how to breathe. Home by early evening to handle some spreadsheet stuff and prep for the week ahead. Tired in the best way.
thought_process: Today felt like a gift I gave myself. No studio, no clients, no decisions about ink and skin. I needed this kind of slowness after what's been a heavy month. The weather was too perfect to waste, and I realized early on that I wasn't in the mood for productivity—I was in the mood for wandering. Capitol Hill, the vintage shops, the ice cream, it all felt intentional without being planned. Even watching the lake traffic from Gas Works was meditative; I could feel the city's pleasure in the day without being pulled into it. By the time I got home, I was ready to handle the mundane stuff—bills, prep work—because I'd actually recharged instead of just collapsed. Sometimes you don't need adventure; you need permission to just exist.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
